purpose: Reference for an agent to (a) attach friendly tags next to resolved model names found in Copilot Dash and (b) diagnose which Outlook Agent routing path a conversation took, to help isolate where quality issues are occurring.

context.system_overview: The Outlook Agent uses a Switcher routing layer inside Sydney's outer loop. On each turn, a small classifier model decides whether to handle the query on the Sydney/Tools branch (Mainline-equivalent, with optional WorkBerry agentic search) or to hand off to the CodeGen container (OPG codegen via outlook_container_handoff). Switcher and CodeGen are HARNESSES/PATHS, not models. The resolved model names in Copilot Dash tell you which path actually executed on a given turn.

paths.sydney_tools: Sydney/Tools branch — Mainline BizChat-equivalent path. Top-level model is GPT-5.3 Chat. May call SonicBerry as a sub-tool for WorkBerry agentic search/grounding.
paths.codegen_claude: CodeGen branch via Claude Code SDK harness. Top-level model is Claude Opus 4.6 or 4.7. Frontier-only; will NOT ship to GA.
paths.codegen_ghcp: CodeGen branch via GitHub Copilot (GHCP) harness. Top-level model is GPT-5.5 (or GPT-5.4 mini when the OPG model router picks the cheap branch). This is the GA-bound path.

model_catalog.notes: Small/fast utility model. Runs BEFORE the Sydney-vs-CodeGen split as the classifier (decides simple vs complex). Its presence does NOT, by itself, tell you which branch was taken — always correlate with the other resolved models on the turn.
model_catalog.diagnostic_hint: Expect this on almost every turn. Ignore for path diagnosis; use only the top-level conversational model.

model_catalog.notes: This is what Mainline BizChat ships at 100% WW. When present as the top-level model, the conversation went through the Sydney/Tools branch of Switcher, NOT CodeGen. May also appear as prod-gpt-53-chat-harmony-completions.
model_catalog.diagnostic_hint: If this is the top-level model and there's a quality issue, the problem is on the Sydney/Tools side (prompt, tool definitions, grounding, or 5.3 Chat behavior).

model_catalog.notes: Powers the WorkBerry tool (O365.AgenticSearch — click/scroll/find). Called as a sub-tool by Sydney for deep enterprise search/grounding. Never the top-level conversational model.
model_catalog.diagnostic_hint: Co-presence with prod-gpt-53-chat = Sydney/Tools branch invoked WorkBerry for grounding. If quality issue is about search/retrieval/grounding correctness, this is the layer to investigate.

model_catalog.friendly_tag: Claude Opus 4.6 — CodeGen path (Claude Code harness)
model_catalog.notes: Top-level model inside the OPG codegen container via Claude Code SDK harness. Frontier-only — will NOT ship to GA. GA will migrate this branch to GPT-5.5 via GHCP harness.
model_catalog.diagnostic_hint: Quality issue here = CodeGen branch issue. Check container handoff logic, codegen prompts, tool payloads, and Opus 4.6 behavior. Note: this path is being deprecated for GA.

model_catalog.friendly_tag: Claude Opus 4.7 — CodeGen path (Claude Code harness)
model_catalog.notes: Newer Opus variant used in CodeGen evaluations; same routing implication as 4.6.
model_catalog.diagnostic_hint: Same as Opus 4.6 — CodeGen via Claude Code harness.

model_catalog.notes: Top-level model inside the OPG codegen container via the GitHub Copilot (GHCP) harness. This is the GA-bound CodeGen configuration replacing Opus 4.6.
model_catalog.diagnostic_hint: Quality issue here = CodeGen branch on the GA-bound stack. High-signal: regressions here directly affect GA readiness.

model_catalog.friendly_tag: GPT-5.4 mini — CodeGen path (Office AI model router, cost-optimized branch)
model_catalog.notes: The Office AI model router (built on GPT-5.4 nano) routes ~27% of Outlook benchmark queries to GPT-5.4 mini for ~87% COGS reduction on those queries, with no statistically significant accuracy drop. Used inside the GHCP harness on the CodeGen branch.
model_catalog.diagnostic_hint: Quality issue here = CodeGen branch + cost-optimized routing. Confirm whether router decision was appropriate for the query complexity.

model_catalog.friendly_tag: GPT-5.4 nano — Office AI model router classifier
model_catalog.notes: Runs the Office AI model router itself — classifies each request by category and reasoning level to decide between mini and the larger CodeGen model.
model_catalog.diagnostic_hint: Like prod-gpt-41-mini-04-14, this is a routing utility. Not a direct path indicator on its own.

model_catalog.friendly_tag: GPT-5.4 Chat — Sydney/Tools path (legacy eval variant)
model_catalog.notes: Was used as an Outlook Agent Sydney-side variant during April 2026 evals before standardizing on 5.3 Chat to match Mainline. May appear in older Dash entries.
model_catalog.diagnostic_hint: Treat the same as prod-gpt-53-chat for path diagnosis — Sydney/Tools branch.

path_diagnosis_rules.explanation: Opus is only used as the top-level model when the CodeGen container handles the turn via the Claude Code SDK. Switcher routed this turn to the CodeGen branch.
GPT-5.5 in the resolved models means CodeGen via the GHCP harness — the GA-bound CodeGen configuration.

path_diagnosis_rules.then_path: CodeGen (GHCP harness, GPT-5.4 mini via Office AI model router)
path_diagnosis_rules.explanation: The Office AI model router selected the cheap mini branch for this turn. Still the CodeGen path.

path_diagnosis_rules.then_path: Sydney / Outlook Agent Tools branch + WorkBerry agentic search
path_diagnosis_rules.explanation: Top-level 5.3/5.4 Chat with SonicBerry invoked as a sub-tool indicates Sydney handled the turn and called WorkBerry for enterprise grounding.

path_diagnosis_rules.then_path: Sydney / Outlook Agent Tools branch (no WorkBerry)
path_diagnosis_rules.explanation: Top-level 5.3/5.4 Chat without SonicBerry means Sydney handled the turn using built-in tools only; no agentic search was needed.
Only the classifier/router model is visible. The top-level conversational model is missing from the resolved list — investigate logging gap or pull more data for this turn.

tagging_instructions: When rendering a resolved model name from Copilot Dash, append the matching `friendly_tag` from `model_catalog` immediately next to the raw name.

When multiple resolved models are present on the same turn, evaluate the rules in `path_diagnosis_rules` in order and apply the FIRST matching rule. Show the resulting path summary above the per-model tags.
Always distinguish role: `top_level_conversational` models tell you the PATH; `classifier_router_utility` and `subtool_grounding` models add context but should not be used alone to diagnose the path.
If no rule matches, label the path as 'Unknown — needs investigation' and surface all resolved models for manual review.

guidance_to_surface.sydney_tools: Issue is in the Sydney/Tools branch (Mainline-equivalent). Investigate: tool definitions, prompt config, 5.3 Chat behavior, classification logic that sent the query here.
guidance_to_surface.sydney_tools_with_workberry: Issue is in the Sydney/Tools branch with WorkBerry grounding. Investigate: WorkBerry retrieval quality, grounding data freshness, SonicBerry citations.
guidance_to_surface.codegen_claude: Issue is in the CodeGen branch via Claude Code harness (Opus). NOTE: this path is Frontier-only and will not ship to GA. Lower long-term investment value; still useful for current Frontier DSAT.
guidance_to_surface.codegen_ghcp: Issue is in the CodeGen branch via GHCP harness (GPT-5.5). HIGH PRIORITY — this is the GA-bound CodeGen path.
guidance_to_surface.codegen_ghcp_router: Issue is in the CodeGen branch when the Office AI model router selected the cheap mini branch. Check whether router decision was correct for this query complexity.

supporting_signals_to_check: BotEndpoint: Avalon → Sydney outer-loop is active (expected for any path).
ModelRunner: DEEP_LEO → heavier reasoning runner, common on CodeGen turns.
Invocation/CIQ tags (fetch_file, FileUpload, CIQ:IsTriggered) → complex query likely classified to CodeGen.
OcvFeedbackType: ThumbsDown + Feedback:HasVerbatim → pull verbatim for failure-topic classification.
Ring (WW/Frontier/SDF/MSIT) and Region → scope of impact and which deployment ring is affected.

worked_examples.name: Example A — Sydney/Tools + WorkBerry
worked_examples.diagnosed_path: Sydney / Outlook Agent Tools branch + WorkBerry agentic search
worked_examples.where_problem_likely_lives: Sydney/Tools side. If retrieval or grounding looks off, focus on WorkBerry/SonicBerry; if the answer composition is wrong, focus on 5.3 Chat prompt config.

worked_examples.name: Example B — CodeGen via Claude Code harness
worked_examples.where_problem_likely_lives: CodeGen branch on Opus 4.6 (Frontier-only). Investigate container handoff, codegen prompt, tool payloads. Note: GA migrates this branch off Opus, so prioritize accordingly.

worked_examples.name: Example C — CodeGen GA-bound stack
worked_examples.where_problem_likely_lives: GA-bound CodeGen path — HIGH PRIORITY for GA readiness.
